name: Eostre
description: Eostre, also known as Ostara, is a Germanic goddess associated with spring, dawn, and fertility. Her name is believed to be the origin of the word 'Easter,' the Christian festival celebrating the resurrection of Jesus Christ. While direct evidence of her worship is limited, primarily stemming from the writings of the Venerable Bede in his 'De temporum ratione' (The Reckoning of Time), Eostre's influence is felt through the traditions and symbols associated with springtime celebrations. Bede describes a pagan festival held in April dedicated to Eostre, during which offerings were made to her in hopes of a bountiful spring. She is often depicted as a goddess of light, bringing warmth and renewal after the cold winter months. Some scholars suggest she may be related to other dawn goddesses found in Indo-European mythologies. The rabbit and the egg are common symbols associated with Eostre, representing fertility, new life, and the return of vitality to the earth. Her festival marked a time of rejoicing and anticipation for the coming growing season, crucial for agricultural societies. While specific myths about Eostre are scarce, her essence embodies the themes of rebirth, hope, and the cyclical nature of life, making her a powerful symbol of the spring season. She represents the awakening of the natural world and the promise of abundance after a period of dormancy. Although documentation is limited, the enduring legacy of her name in the Easter holiday suggests a significant role in the religious beliefs of the Germanic peoples.
subtitle: Goddess of Spring, Dawn, and Fertility

keyMyths.title: Bede's Account
keyMyths.description: The Venerable Bede mentions Eostre in his writings, describing a pagan festival held in April dedicated to her. This festival was a time of offerings and celebration of the spring season.

keyMyths.title: The Hare and the Eggs
keyMyths.description: Folklore connects Eostre to the symbols of the hare and the eggs, representing fertility and new life, which are associated with the spring season. These symbols became integrated into Easter celebrations.

keyMyths.title: The Awakening of Spring
keyMyths.description: Eostre's essence is tied to the awakening of spring, the return of light and warmth, and the renewed fertility of the earth. This embodies the cyclical nature of life and the promise of abundance.

sources.author: Venerable Bede
sources.work: De temporum ratione (The Reckoning of Time)
sources.description: The primary historical source mentioning Eostre and her associated festival.
